subject: Careers
topic: Guest Speaker: Coding, Values, and Career Pathways
grade_subject_fit: Fits Grade 8 Career Education by helping students connect a real worker's education, values, daily tasks, and career pathway to their own questions and reflections.
unit_context: A single-period guest speaker lesson for Patrick from BCLC, a senior coder, focused on education, workplace values, coding work, and possible pathways into technology careers.
assignment_purpose: Students prepare respectful questions, listen for evidence about education, values, and job responsibilities, then reflect on what they learned about technology careers and their own possible next steps.
final_evidence_target: A short reflection that connects one thing Patrick shared to the student's own interests, strengths, values, or future learning choices.
student_task_flow: Preview the guest speaker purpose and respectful listening expectations.
Prepare focused questions about education, values, and coding work.
Record short notes during the presentation using the question sheet.
Complete a reflection that connects the speaker's pathway to personal interests, strengths, values, or next steps.
success_criteria: I prepared at least two respectful questions before or during the talk.
I recorded evidence about Patrick's education, values, and job responsibilities.
I connected one idea from the speaker to my own interests, strengths, values, or future choices.
supports_scaffolds: Students can choose from question stems instead of writing all questions from scratch.
The student packet separates before, during, and after sections.
The reflection uses sentence starters and a short structured response pattern.
Reduced-output option allows bullet notes instead of full paragraphs while preserving the same learning target.

differentiation_model.support_pathway: Use the provided question stems, circle or highlight useful ideas during the talk, and complete the reflection with sentence starters or bullet points.
differentiation_model.core_pathway: Prepare original questions, record evidence during the talk, and complete the reflection using specific details from the speaker.
differentiation_model.extension_pathway: Compare Patrick's pathway to another technology or community career and explain what skills, values, or choices overlap.

checkpoint_release_logic: Students prepare at least one question before the speaker begins.
Students record at least three during-speaker notes before starting the final reflection.
Students complete the reflection checklist before submission.
teacher_implementation_notes: Keep the focus on pathway, values, and learning choices rather than on students needing to choose a technology career now.
Before Patrick begins, remind students that respectful questions can be about education, job tasks, values, problem solving, or workplace habits.
During the talk, pause briefly if needed so students can record notes in point form.
After the talk, give students quiet time to complete the reflection before class discussion.
likely_misconceptions: Students may think coding careers require one exact pathway instead of multiple education and experience routes.
Students may focus only on salary or job title instead of values, problem solving, teamwork, and learning habits.
Students may ask broad questions that are hard to answer instead of focused questions.
assessment_focus: Evidence captured from the guest speaker
Reflection connection to interests, strengths, values, or future learning choices

teacher_guide.title: Teacher Guide - Careers 8 Guest Speaker: Patrick from BCLC
teacher_guide.overview: Students listen to Patrick, a senior coder from BCLC, and connect his education, values, and daily work to their own career learning.
teacher_guide.essential_question: What can one person's career pathway teach us about education, values, and work in technology?
teacher_guide.opening_frame: Today is not about deciding your future job. It is about listening for clues: what the work involves, what values matter, and what learning choices can open doors.
learning_goals: I can prepare respectful questions for a guest speaker.
I can identify education, values, and job responsibilities in a real career story.
I can reflect on how a speaker's pathway connects to my own interests, strengths, or next steps.

timing.activity: Welcome students, introduce Patrick from BCLC, and frame respectful listening expectations.
Students complete the before-speaker section and prepare at least one question.
Guest speaker talk and student note-taking. Prompt students to listen for education, values, and job responsibilities.
Student questions. Encourage focused follow-up questions from the question bank.
Quiet reflection, completion checklist, and collection.

teacher_notes: If students struggle to create questions, direct them to choose one question stem and personalize it.
If the talk runs long, protect the final 6-8 minutes for individual reflection.
Use the rubric as a light evidence check, not a high-pressure mark.
Keep teacher-only assessment guidance out of student-facing materials.
teacher_guide.project_prompt: Students will use Patrick's career story to explain how education, values, and daily work responsibilities can connect in a technology career.
matching_bank: education pathway -> courses, training, certifications, practice, mentors
workplace value -> responsibility, teamwork, problem solving, curiosity, communication
coding task -> designing, testing, debugging, improving systems, helping users
personal connection -> interest, strength, value, question, possible next step

model.title: Teacher model for a specific reflection connection
model.sample: Patrick talked about problem solving and learning from errors. That connects to me because I like tasks where I can test an idea, fix it, and try again.

teacher_guide.assessment_focus: Look for specific evidence from the speaker, respectful question preparation, and a realistic personal connection.

task_sheet.title: Student Packet - Guest Speaker: Coding, Values, and Career Pathways
instructions: Before Patrick speaks, prepare at least one respectful question.
During the talk, listen for education, values, and job responsibilities.
After the talk, complete the reflection using specific evidence from what Patrick shared.

tasks.label: Before
tasks.prompt: Circle one area you want to listen for: education, values, coding work, teamwork, problem solving, or advice for students.

tasks.label: Question prep
tasks.prompt: Write one question you could ask Patrick. Use the question bank if you need a starting point.

tasks.label: During
tasks.prompt: Record one thing Patrick shares about education or learning after high school.

tasks.label: During
tasks.prompt: Record one workplace value, habit, or skill that seems important in his job.

tasks.label: During
tasks.prompt: Record one coding or technology task that Patrick describes.

tasks.label: After
tasks.prompt: Star one note you might use in your final reflection.

embedded_supports: Question stem: What helped you learn how to...?
Question stem: What values matter most in your work?
Question stem: What is one problem you solve in your job?
Question stem: What advice would you give Grade 8 students?
success_criteria: I recorded evidence in at least three sections.
I starred one note to use in my reflection.
I completed the reflection checklist before handing it in.

worksheet.title: Guest Speaker Question Sheet - Patrick from BCLC
anchor: A strong question is specific enough for the speaker to answer clearly.
A respectful question focuses on education, values, job tasks, problem solving, advice, or career pathway.
worksheet.tip: Choose one question stem, then add a detail that fits Patrick's work as a senior coder.

questions.q_text: Education: What is one question you have about how Patrick learned the skills for his job?
Values: What is one question you have about the values or habits that matter in Patrick's workplace?
Job tasks: What is one question you have about what a senior coder actually does during a workday?
Advice: What is one question you have about choices Grade 8 students can make now to build useful skills?

self_check: At least one of my questions is specific.
My questions connect to education, values, work, or advice.

final_response_sheet.title: Guest Speaker Reflection - What I Learned
final_response_sheet.prompt: Use one idea Patrick shared to explain what you learned about technology careers, workplace values, or future learning choices.
planning_reminders: Use one specific detail from the talk.
Connect the detail to your own interests, strengths, values, questions, or next step.
Point form is acceptable if your ideas are clear.

render_hints.purpose_line: This reflection is the final evidence for the guest speaker lesson.
render_hints.response_note: One clear idea in each box is enough.
quick_check_items: I used a specific detail from the speaker.
I connected the detail to myself or my learning.
I wrote clearly enough for someone else to understand my thinking.

success_criteria: I named one specific idea from Patrick.
I explained why that idea matters.
I connected the idea to my own interests, strengths, values, or next step.

rubric_sheet.title: Guest Speaker Learning Evidence Rubric
rubric_sheet.purpose_line: Use this rubric to check question preparation, listening evidence, and reflection quality.

criteria.name: Question preparation
criteria.descriptor: The student prepared respectful questions connected to education, values, work, or advice.

criteria.name: Listening evidence
criteria.descriptor: The student recorded specific notes from the guest speaker instead of only general reactions.

criteria.name: Reflection connection
criteria.descriptor: The student connected one speaker idea to personal interests, strengths, values, questions, or next steps.

success_criteria: My question was respectful and connected to the speaker.
My reflection connects the speaker's ideas to my own learning.
